chunks.text: Distinguished Alumni Awards winners are selected each year by the EE and CS Chairs in consultation with the EECS Faculty Awards Committee and with input from the EECS faculty. Winners receive a plaque awarded at a departmental event such as the BEARS conference or the L&S CS Commencement.
chunks.question: How are Distinguished Alumni Award winners selected?
chunks.answer: By EE and CS Chairs with faculty input.

chunks.text: 2025: Dana Angluin (Ph.D. 1976, advisor: Manuel Blum), Professor Emeritus of CS, Yale. John Schulman (Ph.D. 2016, advisor: Pieter Abbeel), Co-Founder of OpenAI and creator of ChatGPT.
chunks.question: Who won the CS Distinguished Alumni Award in 2025?

chunks.text: 2024: Nancy Amato (M.S. 1988, advisor: Manuel Blum), Abel Bliss Professor and CS Department Head, UIUC. Lars Rasmussen (Ph.D. 1998, advisor: Alistair Sinclair), Angel Investor, co-founder of Google Maps, Google Wave, and Weav Music.
chunks.question: Which 2024 award winner co-founded Google Maps?

chunks.text: 2023: Paul Debevec (Ph.D. 1996, advisor: Jitendra Malik), Director of Production Innovative Research, Netflix; Adjunct Research Professor, USC Computer Science. Andrea Arpaci-Dusseau (M.S. 1994/Ph.D. 1998, adviser: David Culler), Professor, University of Wisconsin, Madison. Remzi Arpaci-Dusseau (M.S. 1996/Ph.D. 1999, adviser David Patterson), Professor, University of Wisconsin, Madison.
chunks.question: What is Paul Debevec's role at Netflix?

chunks.text: 2022: Kimberly Keeton (MS 1994/PhD 1999, adviser: David Patterson), Principal Engineer, Google. Noam Nisan (PhD 1988, advisor: Richard Karp), Professor of Computer Science, Hebrew University of Jerusalem.

chunks.text: 2021: Ralph Merkle (BA 1974/MS 1977), Senior Research Fellow, Institute for Molecular Manufacturing. Hari Balakrishnan (PhD 1998, advisor: Randy Katz), Fujitsu Professor of Computer Science and Artificial Intelligence at MIT, Founder/CTO of Cambridge Mobile Telematics.

chunks.text: 2019: Andrew Ng (PhD 2003, advisor: Michael Jordan), Professor, Stanford. Amin Vahdat (BS 1992/PhD 1998, advisor: Thomas Anderson), Engineering Fellow and Vice President, Google.

chunks.answer: Andrew Ng is a Stanford professor.
